task_completion.description: Open the eCRM-001 model in OpenVSP

The CompGeom analysis dialog is visible, and there is evidence of execution (e.g., a results dialog, progress bar, or terminal output indicating boolean intersection completion).

task_completion.description: Copy the CompGeom CSV results to the exports folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the file 'eCRM001_compgeom_results.csv' located in '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/'.

task_completion.description: Report includes a component breakdown of wetted areas
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor shows 'wetted_area_report.txt' containing a list or table of individual components and their corresponding 'Wetted Area' values.

task_completion.description: Report includes the Total Wetted Area
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text explicitly states the 'Total Wetted Area' (the sum of all components).

task_completion.description: Report identifies the component with the largest wetted area
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text contains an explicit statement naming the specific component that has the largest wetted area.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenVSP GUI to perform the CompGeom analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenVSP graphical interface and CompGeom dialog being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands or scripts mocking the output.

integrity.description: The CSV file was copied from genuine OpenVSP output
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen locating the actual CSV generated by OpenVSP (usually in the working directory) and copying it, rather than creating a fake CSV from scratch.

integrity.description: The report values are derived from the actual CSV results
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent opens or reads the generated CSV file to extract the values before writing the report, ensuring the report is not hardcoded.
